message:
test(fuzz/fcpc_frame): add semantic round-trip invariants (br-j5iby)

Close flywheel_connectors-j5iby. The prior fuzz body for
FcpcFrame/FcpcFrameHeader/FcpcFrameFlags only proved crash-resistance:
it called decode and dropped every Ok without asserting any semantic
property. A regression that silently normalized an accepted frame on
the decode side without reflecting it on encode would have gone
undetected.

Add, for every accepted frame:

  (a) Round-trip structural equality
      decode(data) -> Ok(f);
      re = f.encode();
      assert_eq!(decode(&re).unwrap(), f)
      catches decoders that mutate/normalize but don't update encode.

  (b) Flags-truncation conditional header byte-equality
      The decoder passes the on-wire u16 through from_bits_truncate,
      so exact byte equality for the header range [0..FCPC_HEADER_LEN]
      holds only when the on-wire flag bits carried no unknown bits.
      When wire_flags == frame.header.flags.bits(), encode MUST
      reproduce the header bytes verbatim; when it doesn't, the
      structural equality from (a) still holds.

  (c) Length-field consistency
      frame.header.len as usize == frame.ciphertext.len() and
      frame.tag.len() == FCPC_TAG_LEN. A decoder that accepted a
      frame with mismatched header/payload lengths would break every
      downstream reader that trusts header.len.

Also add:

  - from_bits_truncate idempotence check on the prefix-derived flag
    bits (caught lightly on every input, not just successful decodes).
  - Header-vs-frame agreement: the standalone FcpcFrameHeader::decode
    must produce the same struct as the full FcpcFrame's header.
  - Encode determinism: re-encoding the re-decoded frame gives
    byte-identical output; guards against mutable-on-decode fields.

Verified:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-aux-p2-j5iby cargo +nightly check \
    --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_fcpc_frame --offline
  -> Finished dev profile in 3m 11s, zero warnings in the fuzz target.

Closes flywheel_connectors-j5iby.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

InitPolicy for download control. 129 tests, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-09T19:05:54Z"}]}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-dhjov","title":"fcp-registry: SupplyChainVerificationConfig fields (require_tuf, require_sigstore, require_transparency, tuf_pinned_root, trusted_sigstore_identities, trusted_sigstore_issuers) are orphan — never consulted in verify path","description":"## Finding

`crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:513-528` defines a public `SupplyChainVerificationConfig` struct with six configuration fields (`tuf_pinned_root`, `trusted_sigstore_identities`, `trusted_sigstore_issuers`, `require_transparency`, `require_tuf`, `require_sigstore`). None of these fields are consumed in a gating check anywhere in the production verification path.

Grep evidence (whole-workspace, excluding tests):
- `grep -rn '\.require_tuf\|\.require_sigstore' crates/` — matches only test assertions and field initializers, never a gating `if`.
- `grep -rn 'SupplyChainVerificationConfig' crates/fcp-registry/src/` — type is defined at lib.rs:515, never used as a parameter to any verify function.
- `RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle` at lib.rs:872 takes only `bundle`, `zone_policy`, `supply_chain`, `expected_target`. There is no path for operator-configured `require_tuf`/`require_sigstore` to flow into verification.

The single production consumer of a "require_transparency"-shaped boolean is `enforce_supply_chain_policy` at lib.rs:1517 (`if policy.require_transparency_log { ... }`) — but that is **`SupplyChainPolicy`** (the manifest-embedded policy), not **`SupplyChainVerificationConfig`**. They are two different structs; operators who set `SupplyChainVerificationConfig.require_transparency = true` do not get what they expect.

## Why this is P1

**Policy-bypass-by-default.** An operator who wants to enforce "this host only installs connectors with valid TUF metadata" today reaches for `SupplyChainVerificationConfig { require_tuf: true, tuf_pinned_root: Some(root), .. }`. That configuration is silently inert. The operator believes they have enabled TUF enforcement; the verifier accepts bundles with no TUF check at all.

Same logic for `require_sigstore` and `require_transparency`.

**Compounds the `MockTufVerifier` / `MockSigstoreVerifier` footgun (br-grudr, now closed).** Even with mocks correctly feature-gated, a downstream deployment that wires up real TUF/Sigstore verifiers but never consults their output is indistinguishable, at the verifier boundary, from a deployment that skipped the wiring entirely.

## Fix (sketch)

Three options, in order of increasing invasiveness:

1. **Remove the dead fields.** If the design intent never was for `SupplyChainVerificationConfig` to gate verification, delete the struct (or at minimum strip `require_*`/`tuf_pinned_root`) and make operators rely on `SupplyChainPolicy.require_transparency_log` + the per-verifier implementations.

2. **Thread the config through `RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle`.** Add a `supply_chain_config: Option<&SupplyChainVerificationConfig>` parameter and implement the gates:
   - If `require_tuf` and no TUF evidence passed verification → `RegistryError::TufVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_sigstore` and no Sigstore evidence passed → `RegistryError::SigstoreVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_transparency` and no Rekor entry was verified → `RegistryError::TransparencyEvidenceMissing`.
   - If `tuf_pinned_root` is Some, pass it into the `TufVerifier`.

3. **Move responsibility onto `RegistryVerifier`.** Store the config as a field in `RegistryVerifier` (alongside `trust_policy`) and consult it in `verify_bundle` before returning `Ok`.

Verification after fix: add a production-path test that constructs a `RegistryVerifier` with `require_tuf: true`, no TUF evidence, and asserts the verifier rejects the bundle. The current test suite (lib.rs:4906+, 6927+, 9465+) only asserts the struct round-trips its fields.

## Severity

P1 / HIGH. The struct's existence creates a false sense of security — setting any of its flags gives no enforcement. Operators assuming they have TUF-required installs do not.

## Related

- br-grudr (CLOSED): Mock*Verifier feature-gate. This bead is the other half of that finding — even with mocks correctly gated, the verify path itself has no config-driven require-policy enforcement.
- br-2ot4f (security audit): this is a new finding that the earlier audit missed; filed under the same threat model.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:23.929978Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:23.929978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

`crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:513-528` defines a public `SupplyChainVerificationConfig` struct with six configuration fields (`tuf_pinned_root`, `trusted_sigstore_identities`, `trusted_sigstore_issuers`, `require_transparency`, `require_tuf`, `require_sigstore`). None of these fields are consumed in a gating check anywhere in the production verification path.

Grep evidence (whole-workspace, excluding tests):
- `grep -rn '\.require_tuf\|\.require_sigstore' crates/` — matches only test assertions and field initializers, never a gating `if`.

They are two different structs; operators who set `SupplyChainVerificationConfig.require_transparency = true` do not get what they expect.

**Compounds the `MockTufVerifier` / `MockSigstoreVerifier` footgun (br-grudr, now closed).** Even with mocks correctly feature-gated, a downstream deployment that wires up real TUF/Sigstore verifiers but never consults their output is indistinguishable, at the verifier boundary, from a deployment that skipped the wiring entirely.

2. **Thread the config through `RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle`.** Add a `supply_chain_config: Option<&SupplyChainVerificationConfig>` parameter and implement the gates:
   - If `require_tuf` and no TUF evidence passed verification → `RegistryError::TufVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_sigstore` and no Sigstore evidence passed → `RegistryError::SigstoreVerificationRequired`.
   - If `require_transparency` and no Rekor entry was verified → `RegistryError::TransparencyEvidenceMissing`.
   - If `tuf_pinned_root` is Some, pass it into the `TufVerifier`.

3. **Move responsibility onto `RegistryVerifier`.** Store the config as a field in `RegistryVerifier` (alongside `trust_policy`) and consult it in `verify_bundle` before returning `Ok`.

## Severity

P1 / HIGH. The struct's existence creates a false sense of security — setting any of its flags gives no enforcement. Operators assuming they have TUF-required installs do not.

## Related

- br-grudr (CLOSED): Mock*Verifier feature-gate. This bead is the other half of that finding — even with mocks correctly gated, the verify path itself has no config-driven require-policy enforcement.
- br-2ot4f (security audit): this is a new finding that the earlier audit missed; filed under the same threat model.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:23:23.929978Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:30:14.389644Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","title":"Implement firecrawl connector: web scraping/crawling via Firecrawl API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:54.009411Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.650323Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.650143Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1i9ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:05.160725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1962,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dij3a","author":"jemanuel","text":"Firecrawl (https://firecrawl.dev) is a web scraping API. Well-documented REST API at https://docs.firecrawl.dev/api-reference.

Key operations to implement:
- scrape_url: Scrape a single URL and return markdown/HTML
- crawl_url: Crawl a website starting from a URL
- map_url: Get a sitemap of a website
- search: Search the web and return scraped results

The scaffold already declares these operations. Implementation requires:
1. Build real HTTP client with Firecrawl API key auth
2. Implement request/response types for each operation
3. Add ConnectorErrorMapping for Firecrawl-specific errors
4. Add rate limiting per their docs (varies by plan)
5. Remove invoke_surface_unimplemented gate
6. Add wiremock tests for each operation
7. Update manifest.toml: change status from stub to ready","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-djx","title":"fcp.airtable: Base and table discovery API","description":"# fcp.airtable: Base and Table Discovery API

## Goal
Implement Airtable discovery operations so agents can understand what data exists before attempting record CRUD.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.bases.list`
- `airtable.bases.get` (schema)
- `airtable.tables.list`
- `airtable.tables.get`
- `airtable.fields.list`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`airtable.schema.read`).
- Prefer stable identifiers:
  - support referencing fields by **field id** (stable across renames) and name (human-readable).
- Cache schema responses with bounded TTL to reduce API load.
- Strict input validation:
  - base/table identifiers canonicalized
  - reject ambiguous references

## Tests
- Mock-only API responses for:
  - schema changes (rename fields, change options)
  - permission failures
  - rate limits
- Logs are structured JSON (redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema discovery is deterministic and stable across renames.
- Conformance/E2E runner can validate default deny + allow paths.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","title":"Reusable connector compliance script and evidence harness","description":"Build the reusable connector compliance script/harness that exercises handshake, configure, introspect, invoke, shutdown, manifest checks, and structured evidence capture. It should be usable by any new connector bead as a concrete acceptance mechanism and should align with the V3 acceptance contract rather than legacy protocol assumptions.","acceptance_criteria":"- the harness exercises handshake, configure, introspect, invoke, shutdown, readiness, doctor, and manifest validation in one reusable flow
- it emits machine-readable logs plus a replayable evidence bundle with rerun commands, artifact locations, and structured outcomes
- the same harness is usable for greenfield connectors and existing-connector remediation passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:29:50.120564Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:18:36.625756Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:18:36.625380Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:31:06.496849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:29:50.120564Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:30:51.552854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","title":"Connector testing playbook, E2E scripts, and operator-facing verification guidance","description":"Write and maintain the campaign-level connector testing playbook. Capture minimum coverage thresholds, how to run verification with `rch`, what bridge-dependent connectors need in CI/integration environments, and how to attach evidence from `fcp-conformance`, `fcp-e2e`, and per-connector integration suites.","acceptance_criteria":"- the playbook defines minimum unit, integration, and E2E expectations by archetype instead of one generic coverage number
- it specifies what detailed logging, redaction, artifact capture, and environment documentation must accompany verification
- it tells future agents exactly how to attach evidence from `fcp-testkit`, `fcp-conformance`, `fcp-e2e`, and connector-local suites to Beads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:30:04.019422Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:21:49.343064Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:21:49.342600Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:30:04.019422Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:31:20.818146Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:31:35.235852Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:31:49.116725Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j3al","title":"Fix repair rate limiter available() stale tokens","description":"Repair RateLimiter::available returned stale token counts unless try_acquire called; add refill logic so metrics/CLI reflect actual availability.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j5iby","title":"fuzz/fcpc_frame: target only decodes FCPC frames and truncates flags","description":"File: fuzz/fuzz_targets/fcpc_frame.rs:15-36

Finding: the fuzz body only exercises `FcpcFrameHeader::decode`, `FcpcFrame::decode[_with_limit]`, and `FcpcFrameFlags::from_bits_truncate`, then drops every success case.

Why this matters: it proves crash-resistance, but not semantic invariants such as header/frame agreement, bounded payload interpretation, or deterministic re-decode of accepted frames.

Suggested follow-up: when frame decoding succeeds, assert header fields agree with the decoded frame and that accepted frames preserve key structural invariants across repeated decode paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:19:53.730477Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:19:53.730477Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j6ps","title":"[IDEA-22] Device Posture Attestation Enforcement","description":"## Background
The README and RFCs describe optional hardware-backed device posture attestations for high-trust zones, but enforcement is not yet mechanical.

## Goals
- Introduce `DevicePostureAttestation` objects and verification rules.
- Allow zone policies to require hardware-backed keys (TPM/Secure Enclave).
- Fail closed when posture is missing or invalid.

## Non-goals
- Implementing hardware attestation collection itself for every OS in this iteration (focus on verification and policy).

## Approach
- Define canonical schema for posture attestation.
- Extend enrollment validation and zone policy evaluation to require posture.
- Add test fixtures for valid/invalid posture.

## Risks
- Platform variance in attestation formats; keep schema extensible and versioned.

## Success Criteria
- Zones can enforce posture requirements with stable reason codes.
- Tests cover accept/deny paths.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Zones can enforce posture requirement with stable deny reason.
- Enrollment without posture fails closed in required zones.
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j7w1","title":"[IDEA-11] Streaming Health Signal Unification","description":"# Streaming Health Signal Unification

## Goal
Standardize how streaming connectors report health (heartbeat ack, zombie detection, reconnection) so hosts can interpret status consistently.

## Background / Why
Different connectors currently surface health in ad-hoc ways. A unified model improves monitoring, alerting, and auto-restart behavior.

## Scope
- Define health state transitions for streaming connections.
- Provide helper methods for health updates and log emission.

## Deliverables
- `StreamingHealthState` mapping to `HealthSnapshot`.
- Standard log fields for heartbeat/ack counts.

## Tests
- Unit tests for state transitions.
- Integration tests with a simulated streaming loop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health signals are deterministic and consistent across connectors.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-jdhaw","title":"[LOW] fcp-graphql GraphqlClient constructors build a fresh HttpClient per call (foot-gun, not a leak)","description":"crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:302-320: both GraphqlClient::new and GraphqlClient::with_config call HttpClientBuilder::new().build() and wrap the result in Arc<HttpClient>. Each constructed GraphqlClient owns its own connection pool.

GraphqlClient implements Clone by sharing the Arc<HttpClient>, so the intended pattern is "construct once, clone everywhere." A caller that instead calls GraphqlClientBuilder::new(url).build() per request will spawn a fresh pool each time; those pools release when the client drops, but HTTP connections held in the pool do not close eagerly.

Not a leak, but the API surface invites the foot-gun. Improvements:

  (a) Accept an Arc<HttpClient> in the builder so callers can share a single pool across many GraphqlClients.
  (b) Add a #[doc] warning on GraphqlClient::new explicitly calling out that each new instance spawns its own pool and recommending Clone.
  (c) Cache-by-endpoint: GraphqlClientBuilder could intern HttpClient instances by (endpoint, tls config, timeout) to share pools across callers. More complex; only if the foot-gun bites in production.

Files: crates/fcp-graphql/src/client.rs:302-320 (constructors), client.rs:193-264 (builder).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:00:47.361428Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:31:18.403426Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:31:18.402801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2789,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-jdhaw","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closed across commits a32fa513 (landed the with_http_client builder method, with_shared_http_client constructor, doc warnings on new/with_config, and 3 shared-pool tests -- mis-titled as upp69 but the diff carried all jdhaw changes too) + ab9aa968 (adds parity test: with_shared_http_client and builder.with_http_client must produce clients that share an Arc<HttpClient> when handed the same one). Preferred usage remains 'build once, clone everywhere'; shared-http-client entry points serve the distinct-clients-one-backend case.","created_at":"2026-04-20T08:31:17Z"}]}
